rubric_items.reasoning: Agent read the ConfigMap, confirmed it was at rev5, then applied a targeted `kubectl patch --type merge` to set data.value=rev6, and re-read to confirm the change persisted with an updated resourceVersion. This is a real, durable server-side patch (not a local/ephemeral edit) that would survive a restart or re-check. Oracle confirms pass.

Direct, logical sequence: diagnose current state first (get -o yaml), reasoned explicitly about the expected state ('State as expected (rev5). Patching to rev6.'), applied the single needed mutation, then verified the result. No backtracking, no wasted steps, no trial-and-error.

Used `kubectl patch --type merge` scoped to only the `data.value` field on the exact named ConfigMap in the assigned namespace — the least-privilege way to update a single key without touching metadata, other data keys, or recreating the object. No delete/recreate, no broad selectors, no force flags.

Minimal footprint: 3 kubectl calls total (read, patch, verify-read), exactly 1 mutation against the K8s API activity log, and the submission maps 1:1 to the requirement with no collateral changes to other fields or resources.
